title: Employer-sponsored retirement plans
description: Practice understanding of retirement plans 401(k), Roth 401(k), Simple IRA, SEP IRA, TSP, 403(b), 457, and pension.

assessment_items.question_content:
Most people save for retirement through a 401(k) plan at work.

**What advantages does a 401(k) have over an IRA account?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your employer can match what you put in.
You have complete control over the account.

assessment_items.question_content:
You want to start a 401(k) retirement account. 

**How to do you get a 401(k)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by setting it up on your own online
only if your employer offers it at work
by asking your local bank to open one

assessment_items.question_content:
Your job offers both a 401(k) and a Roth 401(k).

**If you choose the traditional 401(k), when do you pay taxes on your money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when you put the money in
every year on how much the money grows
only when you take the money out in retirement

assessment_items.question_content:
You're deciding between a Roth 401(k) and a Roth IRA.

**What makes Roth 401(k) a better choice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your employer can match what you put in.
You pay less in taxes when you retire and take the money out.
You have complete control over the account.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're interested in having a Roth 401(k).

**How do you add money to a Roth 401(k)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: directly from your paycheck with after-tax dollars
from your personal savings or checking account
with funds from your tax return each year
depositing cash directly at a bank branch

assessment_items.question_content:
Your job offers both a 401(k) and a Roth 401(k).

**If you choose the Roth 401(k), when do you pay taxes on your money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you pay taxes when you put the money in
every year on how much the money grows
only when you take the money out in retirement

assessment_items.question_content:
You work for a small company that offers a Simple IRA as their retirement plan. You decided to contribute a part of your paycheck.

**When do you pay taxes on Simple IRA contributions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You pay taxes on them now.
You never pay taxes on them.
You pay taxes when you take the money out.

assessment_items.question_content:
A Simple IRA can be a great way to save for retirement. 

**Who is a Simple IRA especially designed for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: large corporations with thousands of employees
non-profit organizations with any number of employees
small businesses with $100$ or fewer employees

assessment_items.question_content:
You are a small business owner and have decided to offer a Simple IRA to your employees.

**Who must contribute to the Simple IRA?**

The [[☃ dropdown 1]] contributes automatically, while the [[☃ dropdown 2]] can choose to contribute.
hints: Consider who sets up the Simple IRA and may have obligations to support it financially.
Employers often provide benefits to encourage employee participation in retirement savings.
All retirement plans are always optional to employees, so employees only contribute if they choose to.
The $\boxed{\text{employer}}$ contributes automatically, while the $\boxed{\text{employee}}$ can choose to contribute.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're self-employed and want a retirement plan that lets you make large contributions with minimal hassles. You also want something that's easy to manage and offers tax benefits.

**Which retirement plan best fits your needs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are self employed and have no employees. You would like to open a retirement savings account.

**Which of these retirement accounts are available to you?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a self-employed individual, you're exploring retirement accounts for their benefits.

**What is a key advantage of a SEP IRA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: larger contributions compared to some other IRAs

assessment_items.question_content:
403(b) plans and 401(k) plans are almost identical in nature, but 403(b) plans are only available for employees of certain organizations.

**Who are 403(b) plans for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

If you're a teacher or work for a nonprofit, your employer probably won't offer a 401(k).

**Which retirement plan will they likely offer instead?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A 403(b) plan is a lot like another popular retirement plan.

**Which plan is it similar to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

457 retirement plans are available to state and local government workers.

**What makes a 457 plan different from other retirement plans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It provides a guaranteed lifetime income.
You can take all of your money out early, with no penalty if you leave your job.

assessment_items.question_content:
You used to work for the state and had a 457 plan. Now you have a new job.

**When can you take money out of the 457 plan without penalties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A 457 plan offers unique flexibility for withdrawing funds.

**When can you take money out of a 457 plan without a penalty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: after you leave the job where you had the 457 plan

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the unique features of a pension is the retirement benefits it gives.

**What kind of benefit does a pension give?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Pensions stand out because of their unique way of providing and funding retirement income.

**Who typically pays for the entire pension plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: your employer pays for all of it

assessment_items.question_content:
Pensions are not as common as they used to be.

**Who is most likely to get a pension plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
